●Meet Philip Howard — Owner & President

A Third-Generation Builder Who Treats Your Home Like His Own

Philip Howard is a Sonoma County native and third-generation builder who has personally led every Howard Construction project since founding the company in 2000. His grandfather was a carpenter. His father was a carpenter. Phil has 25+ years of hands-on Northern California construction experience.

When you hire Howard Construction, you hire Phil directly. He visits your home, writes your estimate, supervises your build, and walks your final punch list. No middlemen. No surprises. No excuses.

"My grandfather always said: your reputation is built one nail at a time. I’ve never forgotten that."
Meet Philip Howard →

Full-Service Construction
& Remodeling

Home Remodels

Pool & Outdoor

Garages & Utility

🏡
ADU Construction

Full ADU builds across all Northern California counties from permits to occupancy.

  • Detached & attached ADUs

Why Howard Construction
Built on Integrity.
Finished with Precision.
01
Transparent Pricing

Detailed written estimates. No change orders without your written approval. No hidden fees.

02
On-Time Delivery

Realistic timelines we meet. Nightly cleanup, professional crews every day.

03
Licensed & Insured

CA Class B license. Full liability and workers’ comp on every project.

04
Phil Answers His Phone

No call center. You reach the decision-maker directly, always.

We are Northern California construction specialists with deep knowledge of permit offices in Santa Rosa, Napa, Fairfield, San Francisco, and every county we serve. We know the local codes and the subcontractors worth trusting.

Our most-cited attribute across all reviews is not the quality of the finish work — it is that we communicate proactively. You always know exactly where your project stands.

How It Works
Our Simple 5-Step Process

Clear, predictable, no surprises — from first call to final walkthrough.

1
Free Consultation

Phil visits your home and listens to your vision.

2
Detailed Estimate

Written line-item pricing. No vague numbers.

3
Permits & Planning

We file all permits and schedule your start date.

4
Construction

Daily updates, clean site, professional crew.

5
Final Walkthrough

We don’t leave until every item is resolved.

FAQ
Frequently Asked Questions

Answers to the questions we hear most from homeowners across Northern California.

Visit CSLB.ca.gov and look up the license number. Confirm it is active, Class B, and insurance is current. A legitimate contractor provides it immediately.
Yes — get 2 to 3 detailed bids. Never automatically choose the lowest. Compare line by line and ask what is NOT included in each bid.
Mid-range typically runs $40,000–$90,000. High-end custom kitchens reach $120,000–$200,000+. We provide line-item estimates so every dollar is accounted for.
In Northern California, additions typically cost $300–$550 per sq ft for standard work, $600–$900+ for high-end finishes.
The industry standard is 10–20% above the quoted price. We communicate all unexpected discoveries immediately and never proceed without your written authorization.
Cosmetic work typically does not need a permit. Moving walls, plumbing, or electrical does. Unpermitted work can affect insurance and create problems at resale.
Building, Electrical, Plumbing, and Mechanical permits are typically required. We handle the entire submission and follow-up across all Northern California counties.
A standard kitchen takes 4–8 weeks from demo to punch list. We pre-order all long-lead materials before demo begins so nothing waits on parts.
Yes — we have extensive experience making this work. Daily dust barriers, nightly cleanup, and noise schedules. We have completed full remodels with families living on-site throughout.
Philip Howard personally oversees every project. You always have Phil’s direct cell number — no call center, no buffer. You reach the decision-maker directly.
We stop, photograph the issue, and call you immediately. We explain options and costs and do not proceed without written authorization. Transparency is non-negotiable.
All projects include a 1-year workmanship warranty. California law also provides a 10-year structural defect warranty on new construction. All documentation provided at completion.
Every sub is licensed, bonded, and insured in California. We work with trusted specialists we have partnered with for 10+ years and verify CSLB status before every project.
